findings.message: Adds "email" to frontend_rum_allowed_query_params, causing the user's email address (PII) to be collected from the query string and captured into RUM/telemetry. The surrounding allowlist exists for non-PII UTM attribution params only; email is not needed for attribution and the file's own comment plus the added inline comment confirm it is PII flowing into telemetry. Email in telemetry is logged/retained PII collected beyond the stated attribution purpose.
findings.suggestion: Remove the "email" entry from frontend_rum_allowed_query_params. Keep the allowlist limited to non-PII attribution keys (utm_*). If user identity is genuinely required for an attribution use case, capture a non-reversible pseudonymous id server-side under a documented lawful basis and retention limit, never the raw email from the query string.
